design_philosophy.core_loop: Select 3-character team, compete in tournament bracket against rival teams, build power gauge through combat to unleash Super Special Moves
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Hand-to-hand combat mastery balanced against managing power gauge economy and counter-techniques; shift from projectile-heavy to execution-focused gameplay
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Assemble a dream team of fighters and prove dominance in a prestigious martial arts tournament while uncovering a supernatural conspiracy
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately de-emphasized projectiles in favor of rush-down hand-to-hand combat, introducing emergency evasion and counter-mechanics. This created gameplay imbalance as new characters' moves didn't cleanly integrate projectile and melee systems. The game prioritizes aggressive, positioning-based fighting over spacing and zoning

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player selects 3-character team composition; character selection significantly impacts matchup difficulty and available tools

progression.upgrade_system: Power Gauge system: fills during combat, enables Super Special Moves at any power level, or more powerful 'Desperation Moves' when gauge is maxed AND life is critical (flashing red)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Chizuru Kagura and Goenitz (final boss) become playable after tournament completion
replayability_hooks: Unlockable boss characters with unique movesets

world_building_techniques: Tournament bracket structure establishes rival team encounters
Character roster changes communicate narrative shifts (Yuri replaces Takuma, Leona joins Ikari Team)
Boss Team composition references Fatal Fury and Art of Fighting universes (cross-franchise antagonists)
Final boss encounter with Goenitz reveals supernatural threat (Orochi demon sealing subplot)

boss_design.philosophy: Final boss Goenitz serves narrative purpose as Orochi servant attempting to prevent sealing ritual. Boss encounters are tournament-structured rather than isolated gauntlets
notable_bosses: Goenitz (final boss: servant of Orochi demon, tornado-based combat, defeated via tournament bracket or fought directly with Kyo/Iori alliance)

faction_indicators: Yagami Team (Iori, Mature, Vice): rival clan seeking to prevent sealing
Kusanagi Team (Kyo): protagonist protagonist bloodline faction
Boss Team (Geese, Krauser, Mr. Big): cross-franchise villain coalition
Ikari Team: military faction (Leona replacing Heidern)
danger_signals: Goenitz's emergence signals Orochi threat awakening
Tournament by Chizuru (rather than Rugal) indicates power shift in fighting world
Mature and Vice's transition from Rugal's assistants to Iori's team suggests realignment
environmental_storytelling: Tournament bracket progression reveals which clans are active (Kusanagi, Yagami, Yata)
Team compositions show generational changes (Yuri replacing Takuma, Leona replacing Heidern)
Boss Team's inclusion of Fatal Fury/Art of Fighting villains grounds KOF in shared SNK universe
